courses.title: Spreadsheets II
courses.description: This course provides instruction and training in the use of spreadsheet software at the intermediate level. Students will produce sophisticated spreadsheet documents for a variety of business applications. Topics include working with lists and pivottables, consolidating multiple worksheets and workbooks, integrating the spreadsheet software with other software programs and the web, and creating macros. ACCT 112.2 is also listed as CIM 112.2; credit will be given in either area, not both.

sections.notes: For Online: For Online: This course will be completed entirely online. This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, Microsoft 365 will be used.

This class will be taught ONLINE. Uses Microsoft Excel 2021/365.This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, third party applications may be used.

courses.title: Introduction to Personal Computer Applications
courses.description: This course introduces Microsoft Office Professional applications. Topics covered include word processing (Word), spreadsheet (Excel), database (Access), presentation (PowerPoint), email management (Outlook), and World Wide Web navigation software. (Formerly known as CIM 107).

sections.notes: For Online: This course will be completed entirely online. This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, third party applications may be used.
This class will be taught ONLINE and uses Microsoft 365. This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, third party applications may be used.

courses.title: Real Estate Salesperson's License Exam Preparation
courses.description: This course covers fundamental information concerning the practice of real estate and assists those preparing for the real estate salesperson license examination. Emphasis is placed on real estate licensing, contract law, agency law, taxation, appraisal, and escrow.

sections.notes: This is a 2-week, fully online class, requiring NO face-to-face meetings. In this accelerated format, active, weekly online participation is required for success. This class utilizes Canvas, accessible from the IVC homepage, and a companion web system from the book publisher. Students must log-in to Canvas by Tuesday of the first week of class to download/view the syllabus. If the syllabus is not downloaded by Tuesday, the student may be dropped. Look for further directions from your teacher the week class begins.

courses.title: Introduction to Cloud Computing
courses.description: This course covers cloud deployment and service models, cloud infrastructure, and the key considerations in migrating to cloud computing. This course also provides the required technology essentials across all domains, including compute, storage, networking, applications, and databases, to help develop a strong understanding of virtualization and cloud computing technologies.The course also surveys cloud careers and discusses industry demandfor cloud skills.

sections.notes: For Online: This course will be completed entirely online. This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, third party applications may be used.

courses.title: UC Student Education Plan
courses.description: This course covers important information for students who plan to transfer to one of the nine University of California campuses. Topics include admission requirements, general education options, transfer admission guarantee programs, the application process, and helpful online resources. Each student will develop a comprehensive student education plan that includes needed courses for each semester.

sections.notes: This is an online class with all course requirements completed via Canvas. There are no required scheduled live stream meetings. Optional live-stream meetings may be held. This class is being taught via Canvas at https://ivc.instructure.com (also available from the IVC homepage). Further information about your course will be available therein. If you are unable to login, contact the Help Desk at (949) 451-5696.

courses.title: Introduction to College Study Skills
courses.instructor: Vanessa Danielle Marquez, Eric Richard Garcia, Javier G Valdez, Marianne Louise Wolfe
courses.description: This comprehensive course is designed to cultivate the holistic student in developing effective academic study skills and critical thinking strategies necessary to reach desired educational goals. Topics surveyed include various educational learning theories; successful interpersonal communication techniques, sensitivity to diverse populations and examination of individual morals and values. Keystone to this course, students learn academic survival strategies such as principles of memory and learning, motivation and concentration, effective note-taking, textbook study methods, efficient time management and test-taking/test anxiety reduction. Emphasis is given to personal growth and development with academic, interpersonal, and professional success.

courses.title: Theory of Coaching
courses.instructor: Michael Aram Bagatourian, Alexander J. Agortsas
courses.description: This generic foundations course is designed for individuals interested in coaching sports at various levels. The course examines the philosophy of coaching; the role and responsibilities of the modern coach; techniques for motivating and communicating with athletes; principles and methods of training; developing and organizing sports programs; nutrition for health and performance; and managing injuries and emergencies.

sections.notes: This course requires the use of Canvas and a device with reliable access to the internet. Additionally, third party applications may be used.Please review course syllabus and complete Module 1 assignment, including orientation, by designated due date.

courses.title: Earth's Natural Environment Laboratory
courses.description: Laboratory exercises and experiments designed to explore and understand the primary areas of physical geography. Stresses the scientific method in interpreting Earth-sun relations; time; Earth representation through globes, topographic maps and remote sensing; meteorological tools, models and weather prognostication; climate; natural vegetation; geomorphologic models and processes, and landform interpretation.

courses.title: Latinx Experience and Success in Higher Education
courses.description: Students will be exposed to practices that contribute to a successful experience in higher education for Latinx students. This course will cover theoretical and empirical overviews of the Latinx educational experience in the United States and the relation of that experience to current educational conditions and trends.

courses.title: Introduction to the Principles of Sustainability
courses.description: This course lays the foundation for the study of global sustainability. With universal foundation/framework, this course provides students with a basic understanding of environmental systems and interrelationship and effect of humans upon the environment. Topics include a historical overview of sustainability and the current problems and issues related to establishing sustainable practices in resource management and conservation of natural resources. Students will examine environmental and urban resource issues, strategies, and tools to investigate and analyze sustainable practices. Students will learn the aspects of systems thinking as it relates to solving sustainability challenges.

sections.notes: This course is part of a College and Career Access Pathway. Enrollment is restricted to CAVA students. Please work with your high school counselor or administrator if you are interested in this class (https://www.ivc.edu/dual-enrollment/ccap). Please DO NOT contact the instructor if you are trying to register, as enrollment starts with the high school.

courses.title: Stress Management
courses.instructor: Vanessa Danielle Marquez, Breanna Roxanne Wybaczynsky, Michelle Marie Minkler, Margaret Marie O'rourke
courses.description: This course introduces the basic principles of holistic stress management. The course explores the mind-body relationship (the psychophysiology) of stress; stressors typically experienced during an individual's college, family and working life; coping skills; and interventions. Students learn cognitive strategies and relaxation techniques that may be applied to help manage daily life stressors in a healthful and productive manner.

courses.title: Women and Work
courses.instructor: Marianne Louise Wolfe, Amanda Ann Romero
courses.description: This course explores common gender issues in the workplace. Within a historical and contemporary framework, it will focus on gender inequality, cultural influences, sexuality, family roles, and ways to address and reduce gender bias in the workplace. Students will also evaluate potential career choices based on self-assessments.

sections.notes: This is an online class with all course requirements completed via Canvas. There are no required scheduled live stream meetings. Optional live-stream meetings may be held. This class is being taught via Canvas at https://ivc.instructure.com (also available from the IVC homepage). Further information about your course will be available therein. If you are unable to login, contact the Help Desk at (949) 451-5696.

This course explores common gender issues in the workplace. Within a historical and contemporary framework, it will focus on gender inequality, cultural influences, sexuality, family roles, and ways to address and reduce gender bias in the workplace. Students will also evaluate potential career choices based on personality, interest, skills, and values assessments.
